intro: The biggest real distinction between Yandex Cloud and Pkl is straightforward: Pkl is free and open-source, while Yandex Cloud is a commercial product (usage-based pricing, a free tier). Yandex Cloud sits at usage-based pricing, a free tier and carries moderate lock-in, while Pkl sits at free and open-source and carries low lock-in. Yandex Cloud is a fit when you need russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities — typical scenarios include russian public cloud with compute, storage, and ml services. Pkl is aimed at high-traffic websites and media-heavy apps, with workloads like automated build/test/deploy pipelines as the common case. The honest trade-off: Yandex Cloud is explicitly not for global startups with western investors or sanctions compliance concerns; Pkl is not intended for apps serving a single geographic region. On cost specifically, pricing model matters more than sticker price — one workload can make Yandex Cloud cheaper while a different shape flips the answer to Pkl.
quick_take: Yandex Cloud is for russian and cis-focused businesses needing local; Pkl is for high-traffic websites and media-heavy apps; decide based on cost model and budget.
verdict_a: Choose Yandex Cloud if your primary workload is russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities.
verdict_b: Choose Pkl if your primary workload is high-traffic websites and media-heavy apps and you want a free/OSS tool with no vendor bill.

faq.q: Is Yandex Cloud cheaper than Pkl?
faq.a: No — Pkl is free and open-source, while Yandex Cloud uses usage-based pricing, a free tier. Pkl wins on sticker price, but remember that self-hosting and maintaining OSS adds engineering overhead that Yandex Cloud's paid plan removes.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Yandex Cloud to Pkl?
faq.a: Migrating from Yandex Cloud to Pkl is doable but not automatic — Yandex Cloud carries moderate lock-in. Since both tools sit in the same category (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), the conceptual mapping is closer, but you still need to validate feature parity against your specific workload. Plan migration as a project, not a script: inventory integrations, test on non-production workloads, and keep Yandex Cloud running in parallel until Pkl is verified.

On our 0–5 developer experience scale, Pkl scores higher (5/5 vs 4/5 for Yandex Cloud). The gap is one point — meaningful but not decisive, since DX is workflow-dependent. Still, score your own tasks: DX is subjective and tied to stack assumptions.

faq.q: Is Pkl a good alternative to Yandex Cloud?
faq.a: Pkl is in the same category as Yandex Cloud (CDN / Edge / Acceleration), so it's a reasonable alternative to evaluate. Pkl is aimed at high-traffic websites and media-heavy apps, while Yandex Cloud targets russian and cis-focused businesses needing local cloud with ml capabilities — that difference should drive the switch decision. Note that Pkl is explicitly not for apps serving a single geographic region — if that describes your workload, it's not a viable replacement.
